title: Warlord of the Empire

{{Quote|I am Grand Admiral Thrawn, Warlord of the Empire, servant of the Emperor.|[[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]], [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]|<ref name="Heir to the Empire">''[[Heir to the Empire]]''</ref>}}
The title of '''Warlord of the Empire''' was a ceremonial title appointed by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to a select few [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]], primarily [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admirals]], [[Grand General/Legends|Grand Generals]], and [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moffs]], although the rank itself was technically higher than these ranks. Similar to the starfighter titles of [[Tan (title)|Tan]] and [[Baron of the Empire/Legends|Baron of the Empire]], Warlord was only recognized by the most skilled and loyal soldiers of the Empire. The title was presented specifically to those Imperial officers who had a particular expressed interest in both ruthlessness and creativity, and were considered not only to repeatedly redefine the impossible, but to do so with inimitable ''panache''. Unlike other standard military ranks, Warlords of the Empire did not have an assigned [[Code cylinder/Legends|code cylinder]] or rank plaque, as it transcended distinctions among the various branches of the Imperial Military, and as such commanded universal respect. The rank was speculated to have been made in homage to the selfsame appellation accorded to those with the rank of [[generalissimi]] from the [[Atrisian Empire]]. Prior to the Empire's sound defeat at Endor, it was considered one of the highest honors the Empire could supply to its forces.

[[Zsinj]] was the first officer to be awarded the rank, with [[Pashna Starkiller]] and [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] also receiving it.

After the events of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] and the disintegration of the Empire as a result of the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] [[Death/Legends|death]], the term ended up being shortened to [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] and being used as a very loose term for various soldiers with nonexistent integrity, due to the term being hijacked by various power-hungry officers in the leaderless vacuum. These false Warlords included small fish such as [[Inos Fonada]] and [[Walang Grazz]], second-tier replacements such as [[Bliss Shargael]] and [[Jmanuel Tethys]], megalomaniacs such as [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Blitzer Harrsk]], ultranationalists such as [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Ennix Devian]], and superlative sadists such as [[Delak Krennel]].
